Moving towards coordinated BIM submission in openBIM format [Updated!] All Submissions that are made in BIM (currently mandatory for new projects with Gross Floor Area (“GFA”) ≥ 5,000 sqm) will need to use the openBIM standard (IFC) and extension IFC+SG catering to local regulatory requirements. Agencies will issue written directions and approvals based on the submitted BIM model in openBIM standard. This seeks to promote upstream design coordination and minimise downstream issues • The “3-Gateways” will be the default submission process for all building projects •

stream issues • The “3-Gateways” will be the default submission process for all building projects • At each Gateway, coordinated BIM submissions (consisting of various building disciplines) will be submitted and reviewed collectively by agencies. Illustration of Design Gateway Considerations • Site layout • Site access points • Analysis of surrounding developments (for instance, if there are emissions) • Building setbacks and buffers • Ancillary structures and communal facilities • Trees to be retained/felled • Parameters for Drainage, Sewerage and Sanitary Works (e.g. MPL, connection points) • Building form, general building layout and development intensity

New RABW - Design gateway details Agency Design Gateway (DG) URA Schematic details of key planning parameters, including • Master Plan parameters (ie. land use/height/intensity) pertaining to the overall building form • Site layout and general building layout • How development relates to surroundings e.g. open spaces, activity generating uses, etc • Connectivity provisions within and around development (indicative location and width) 64

New RABW - Design gateway details Agency Design Gateway (DG) NParks Greenery provision (ie. Green buffers and planting strips) and tree conservation for developments, and any impact to existing parks or green spaces. The model/plans should include: • The spatial provision for greenery • Information of trees/ plants to be conserved (i.e. species, girth, height) along roadside and/or within development boundary. • Elements affecting green buffers and planting strips, including entrance position(s), fire engine accessways, open air parking areas at street level, and other structures (e.g. covered linkways and pedestrian overhead bridges). NParks will also review the conceptual design of any new parks, park connectors or promenades upfront 65

New RABW - Design gateway details Agency Design Gateway (DG) LTA Compliance to traffic operations and safety requirements. Key evaluation areas include:- • Location and provision of access points, pick-up/drop-off and loading/unloading areas • Parking provision and layout • Extent of frontage improvement • Improvement needed to existing traffic scheme • Connections to commuter facilities • Vesting of road reserve plot, if any. Common Misconceptions/Myths about CORENET X [New!] DEBUNKED 1 CORENET X only involves the Qualified Persons. No! Key project members such as Developers, Builders, RE/RTO, Accredited Checkers now have to interact with the system as members of the project team in appointment and submission. 2 All projects must submit in BIM format in CORENET X. No! CORENET X covers both non-BIM and BIM projects. BIM submission is ONLY mandatory for projects with GFA ≥ 5000m2. It is optional for smaller projects to submit in BIM though highly encouraged. For BIM submission in CORENET X, the models will have to be exported to and submit in IFC-SG, an openBIM format. 3 3D BIM submissions and still require 2D drawings to be attached . No! 2D drawings are generally NOT REQUIRED, unless for complex details that ar

ings to be attached . No! 2D drawings are generally NOT REQUIRED, unless for complex details that are impractical or too tedious to be modelled in 3D. For such details, supplementary drawings in 2D may be provided alongside the main BIM model for agencies' review and approval. The CORENET X Code of Practice provides clarity and guidance on details that may be provided in 2D, if they are unable to be provided in BIM. 134

Common Misconceptions/Myths about CORENET X [New!] DEBUNKED 4 All agencies’ requirements must be cleared at the gateways. No! Agencies have reviewed and refined their requirements to ensure alignment across agencies for gateway clearances. Certain detailed and specified requirements can be provided separately to respective agencies for approval e.g. External works, LTA's Transport Impact Assessment, SCDF's performance-based fire safety engineering 5 Demolition works can only happen after the approval of the main building works application. . No! To allow site preparation, application for demolition works can proceed while the design and details of the main building works are being sorted out. 6 Project must restart the process all over if it could not obtain clearance within 2 iterations?

. 6 Project must restart the process all over if it could not obtain clearance within 2 iterations? No! With collective review and collaboration across agencies, submissions should generally not go beyond 2 iterations, unless there are new design changes during resubmission or when the submission quality is lacking. Such submission may require more iterations before clearance may be granted. 135

Common Questions about CORENET X 1 Are pre-submission consultations & waivers still allowed? Yes! Consultations and waiver application are still allowed. CORENET X encourages upfront planning and hence waiver applications should not be an after-thought that is done after plan approval or before TOP/ CSC application. Unlike plan application at the Gateways, waiver applications are not joint submission. Instead, they will be made individually to each agency by the respective QP in accordance with the respective Act and Regulations. 2 Are amendments allowed after submission at each Gateway? Yes! If there is any change in design from the approved plans, the project team will need to submit the Amendment Plans. The project team will have to assess which agencies are affected and require re-appr

mendment Plans. The project team will have to assess which agencies are affected and require re-approval. Similar to today’s practice, amendment plan fees would apply. The amendment submission workflow can be found in the CORENET X Code of Practice. 3 Will TOP/CSC applications be consolidated in a single submission at the Completion Gateway? No! The Completion Gateway will be similar to today’s process where QPs may proceed to clear the respective agency concurrently and independently. The final TOP/CSC will be issued when the project obtains all the necessary clearances from the technical agencies. To improve the user experience, CORENET X will provide a dashboard / checklist for project members to view the status of the TOP/CSC application to each agency. 136

Identifying Approved Plans for Construction Current Practice Identification of Approved Plans via Need for greater ease of verifying/ Checksum on Response Letters identifying approved plans What is Checksum solution? A checksum is an alphanumeric value that uniquely represents the contents of a file Developer QP Site Staff Builder ✓ Digital fingerprint solution Different methods adopted by More secure than traditional watermark different agencies to demarcate approved plans ✓ Easy detection of changes Small changes produce very different looking Difficult to verify whether the checksums plans are indeed the latest approved plans ✓ Files remain intact and can be viewed freely Checksum solution does not affect or encrypt the files 15 1

What are the benefits of using checksum? As compared to the current approach where different agencies adopted different methods to demarcate approved plans, using checksum allows: Benefits Standardised approach • Can be used for all kinds of files (e.g. BIM and 2D files) • Can be used by all agencies to demarcate approved plans Checksum (digital ID) Ease of access and authentication of file embedded in QR code • Files and plans can be viewed without decryption Quick verification of Approved Plan whenever in doubt • Low likelihood of forgery Example of checksum (digital ID) of a file – • Verification can be done easily without specific application, software or internet Note: These images are for illustration purposes only. 15 2

Where can I find the checksums of the approved plans for my project? In the approval response, QP will find a "List of Approved Plans" by each agency. The QR representation of the Approved Plan’s checksum will be listed in the "List of Approved Plans" (1 unique checksum per file).
